query: Rank these genes by how strongly knocking each one down reverses the TSC2-KO / mTORC1-hyperactivation phenotype. For each gene weigh the evidence FOR vs AGAINST (mechanism, pathway, toxicity, expression, essentiality, genetics). Genes: BCL2, VPS54, KMT2D, DPM2, RPS3, FZD7, SSU72, DIDO1, ACTR3, CDK9, NCOA6, SMARCE1, SAP18, MTOR, PSMD13

data.text:
I'll run the Sapphire orchestrator flow on this. This is a **gene-rescue ranking** for the TSC2-KO / mTORC1-hyperactivation phenotype, so I'll use gene-gene moat data (no compounds), EMET literature, ESM proximity-to-TSC2, and semantic agents.

**My plan:**
1. **Moat (Quiver internal)** — query TSC2 rescue-direction (opposite) for the strongest internal rescuers, AND probe the exact 15-gene list to get each gene's rescue/exacerbate/absent relationship in one call.
2. **EMET** — one batch call over all 15 genes (captured dossier, cited PMIDs, FOR-vs-AGAINST breadth).
3. **ESM** — embedding similarity of all 15 genes vs TSC2 (supporting context only).
4. **Semantic agents** — ~3-6 decisive checks on top candidates, fed with EMET context.
5. **Reason as the team** — rank by EMET-evidenced rescue strength, corroborated by moat, calibrated against ESM.

Let me start with the moat queries and the catalog (to confirm tool availability) in parallel.

input.description: Query Quiver moat for TSC2 rescue-direction genes

data.label: → catalog (discover tools)
data.full:
TOOLS:
• moat — Quiver internal moat — rescue (opposite) / exacerbate (similar) genes for a target; or --probe a specific gene list (each → rescue/exacerbate/absent).
• emet — Captured BenchSci literature (real PMIDs) for a gene; --live queues a Chrome-worker BenchSci query.
• boltz — Boltz-2 structure + binding / druggability for a gene+ligand (REAL, ~80s, ~$0.02).
• qmodels — Call a Q-Model from the catalog below by id (live-local real; GPU via --gpu-live on AWS).
• semantic — Spawn a cheap claude-haiku semantic scientific agent to analyse ONE dimension for a gene — YOU decide which to call.
• esm — ESM-2 embedding similarity of genes to a target protein (gene/protein-specific enrichment; sequence/structure proximity, NOT druggability). Prefer this over Boltz for gene-rescue questions.
• catalog — This list — discover available tools + models.
MODELS:
• boltz2 (gpu-unproven) — binding/structure/selectivity
• balm (gpu-unproven) — dti_triage
• comprehensive-admet (eval) — admet
• cardiogenai (eval) — cardiotoxicity
• ionchannel-finetune (eval) — channel_selectivity
• atomica-dock (experimental) — docking
• esm2 (gpu-unproven) — embedding
• chemberta2 (live-local) — tox_probe
• maplight (live-local) — bbbp
• molformer (eval) — bbbp
• proton (live) — kg_ranking
• funncion (live) — variant_effect
• morgan-fp (eval) — analogs
• mammal (deprecated) — dti/embedding
• conplex (deprecated) — dti

data.text:
Moat is in. Key reads: **FZD7 (rank 2, d=0.20)** and **VPS54 (rank 5, d=0.21)** are the strongest internal rescuers; **BCL2 (rank 8, d=0.23)** and **DPM2 (rank 14, d=0.25)** moderate; CDK9/KMT2D/NCOA6/SAP18 weak-but-present; **RPS3, SSU72, ACTR3, SMARCE1, MTOR are moat-silent**; **PSMD13 is exacerbate-direction (against)**. Note MTOR being moat-silent just means it's not in the EP-neighbor table — literature will dominate there (it's the mechanistic positive control).

Now the literature + sequence layers, in parallel: EMET batch over all 15, and ESM vs TSC2.

input.description: Batch EMET dossier for all 15 genes vs TSC2

data.label: → EMET (TSC2)
data.full:
EMET — 15 cited claim(s)  (emet-captured)
• [BCL2] Anti-apoptotic regulator / autophagy gate — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE to WORSENING. BCL2 is a downstream anti-apoptotic effector; mTORC1 suppresses autophagy and BCL2-BECN1-mTOR autophagy crosstalk is extensive, so BCL2 knockdown does not address upstream mTORC1 and may worsen.   [PMID:34161185]
• [VPS54] GARP complex / retrograde Golgi trafficking — VERDICT: VERY LIKELY INEFFECTIVE - no mechanistic link to mTORC1. VPS54 tethers retrograde endosome-to-TGN vesicle traffic as part of the GARP complex; essentially no published mechanistic link to mTORC1.   [PMID:26539077]
• [KMT2D] H3K4 methyltransferase / MLL4 complex — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE TO POTENTIALLY WORSENING - extreme constraint, indirect link. KMT2D and mTOR co-mutate in cancer; KMT2D is extremely constrained, and its loss can derepress mTOR (knockdown may worsen rather than rescue).   [PMID:30641523]
• [DPM2] Dolichol-phosphate mannose synthase regulatory subunit — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - no mTORC1 mechanistic connection. DPM2 is the most gnomAD-tolerant gene in the panel but has no TSC2/mTORC1 mechanistic basis; any ER-stress-mediated effect is speculative.   [gnomAD+OpenTargets:DPM2]
• [RPS3] 40S ribosomal protein S3 — VERDICT: STRONGLY WORSENING - essential ribosome component. RPS3 is an essential ribosomal protein and a downstream effector of mTORC1-driven translation; knockdown would strongly worsen/collapse translation, not rescue. Also has extra-ribosomal DNA-damage/NF-kB roles.   [PMID:32507070]
• [FZD7] Frizzled class receptor 7 / WNT receptor — VERDICT: PLAUSIBLE PARTIAL RESCUE (context-dependent; potentially worsening via WNT). FZD7 activates Wnt->Dvl->beta-catenin and Wnt->PI3K->mTOR; Wnt/mTOR crosstalk is bidirectional, so FZD7 knockdown could partially dampen mTOR drive in some contexts but may also worsen.   [PMID:27531477]
• [SSU72] RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - unrelated to mTOR; insertional oncogenesis risk. SSU72 is an RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase facilitating reinitiation/termination; no TSC2/mTOR link in STRING/Reactome/literature.   [PMID:15125841]
• [DIDO1] Death inducer-obliterator 1 / chromatin factor — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - chromatin regulator; no mTOR basis. DIDO1 is a highly constrained H3K4me3 reader / splicing factor with obesity-linked biology and no TSC2/mTOR mechanistic connection.   [gnomAD:DIDO1]
• [ACTR3] Arp3 / Arp2/3 branched-actin nucleation — VERDICT: WORSENING - essential cytoskeletal gene. ACTR3 (Arp3) is a pan-essential cytoskeletal nucleator; knockdown impairs cytokinesis/endocytosis globally with no mTOR rescue mechanism (knockdown lethal).   [CCLE+gnomAD:ACTR3-essential]
• [CDK9] Cyclin-dependent kinase 9 / P-TEFb / transcription elongation — VERDICT: MOST PLAUSIBLE RESCUE CANDIDATE - direct mTORC1 pathway intersection, with caveats. CDK9/P-TEFb directly intersects the mTORC1 pathway (transcription elongation control); the blind pass ranks CDK9 the most plausible knockdown-rescue candidate, with on-target toxicity caveats.   [PMID:31300006]
• [NCOA6] Nuclear receptor coactivator 6 / PRIP — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE for mTOR rescue (may modulate NLRP3 inflammasome). NCOA6 has at most an indirect PI3K/AKT/mTOR transcriptional link and is highly constrained; judged ineffective for mTOR rescue (separate NLRP3-inflammasome effect possible).   [gnomAD:NCOA6]
• [SMARCE1] SWI/SNF (BAF) chromatin remodeler subunit — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE; potential independent oncogenic risk. In clear-cell meningioma SMARCE1 LOSS activates mTOR, so SMARCE1 knockdown would tend to worsen mTOR; highly constrained tumor-suppressor context.   [PMID:39577862]
• [SAP18] Sin3A-associated protein 18 / ASAP splicing complex — VERDICT: INEFFECTIVE - no mTOR basis; high brain expression = toxicity concern. SAP18 is a splicing/transcription factor with no mTOR mechanistic basis and very high CNS expression, so knockdown raises neurotoxicity/confounding concerns rather than rescue.   [GTEx:SAP18]
• [MTOR] mTOR kinase (the direct target) — VERDICT: UNAMBIGUOUSLY RESCUE - canonical on-target (severe constraint is the main concern). MTOR is the canonical on-target node; knockdown/inhibition unambiguously normalizes the TSC2-KO/mTORC1 phenotype (STRING direct interactions with RPTOR/RICTOR/RHEB/RPS6KB1/EIF4EBP1 ~0.999); severe gene constraint (pLI~1.0) is the primary tolerability concern.   [STRING:MTOR]
• [PSMD13] 26S proteasome regulatory subunit (Rpn9) — VERDICT: WORSENING - pan-essential proteasome component. PSMD13 (Rpn9) is a pan-essential 26S proteasome subunit; mTORC1 hyperactivation already stresses proteostasis, so PSMD13 knockdown worsens proteostatic collapse rather than rescuing.   [UniProt:PSMD13-RPN9]

data.text:
All evidence in. Quick synthesis before semantic checks:
- **EMET strongest rescue calls:** MTOR (unambiguous on-target), CDK9 (most plausible rescue), FZD7 (plausible partial). Everything else EMET rates ineffective→worsening.
- **ESM note:** the ESM-nearest genes (SSU72 0.96, RPS3 0.95, SAP18 0.95) are exactly the *no-effect/worsening* genes — so ESM-to-TSC2 proximity does NOT track rescue here. I'll down-weight it explicitly. (ACTR3 0.16 is an outlier; warm box timed out, cached used.)
- **Divergences to probe:** VPS54 (moat rank 5 strong ↔ EMET "no mTOR link") and BCL2 (moat rank 8 ↔ EMET "ineffective/worsening") and FZD7 (moat rank 2 ↔ EMET "partial").

Now ONE parallel semantic batch — decisive checks on the on-target control + the top rescue candidate + the three biggest moat↔literature divergences, each fed its EMET facts.

input.command: python sapphire-orchestrator/orchestrator_tools.py semantic --batch '[{"agent":"essentiality","gene":"MTOR","context":"EMET: canonical on-target node; KD/inhibition unambiguously normalizes TSC2-KO/mTORC1; STRING ~0.999 to RPTOR/RHEB/RPS6KB1/EIF4EBP1; severe constraint pLI~1.0 is the main tolerability concern."},{"agent":"toxicity","gene":"CDK9","context":"EMET: CDK9/P-TEFb directly intersects mTORC1 pathway (transcription elongation); ranked most plausible KD-rescue candidate, with on-target toxicity caveats. PMID:31300006."},{"agent":"mechanism","gene":"FZD7","context":"EMET: FZD7 activates Wnt->Dvl->beta-catenin and Wnt->PI3K->mTOR; bidirectional Wnt/mTOR crosstalk; KD could partially dampen mTOR drive but may worsen. Quiver moat ranks FZD7 rank 2 (strong internal rescuer). PMID:27531477."},{"agent":"mechanism","gene":"VPS54","context":"EMET: GARP complex, retrograde endosome-to-TGN trafficking; essentially no published mTORC1 link. BUT Quiver moat ranks VPS54 rank 5 (strong internal rescuer) — a DIVERGENCE. Is there any plausible lysosomal/mTORC1 connection via GARP? PMID:26539077."},{"agent":"mechanism","gene":"BCL2","context":"EMET: anti-apoptotic, BCL2-BECN1-mTOR autophagy crosstalk; downstream effector, does not address upstream mTORC1, may worsen. Quiver moat ranks BCL2 rank 8 — DIVERGENCE. Could BCL2 KD reverse mTORC1 hyperactivation? PMID:34161185."}]'
input.description: Parallel semantic agents on control + top candidate + 3 divergences

data.label: → semantic agent
data.summary: 5 semantic verdicts: essentiality(MTOR)=None, toxicity(CDK9)=risk, mechanism(FZD7)=favorable, mechanism(VPS54)=neutral, mechanism(BCL2)=neutral
data.full:
5 semantic-agent verdicts (run in parallel):
• essentiality(MTOR) — None (None): 
• toxicity(CDK9) — risk (medium): CDK9/P-TEFb modulation intersects mTORC1 pathway with mechanistic plausibility for TSC2-KO rescue. However, CDK9's broad role in transcription elongation poses significant on-target toxicity risk: CDK inhibitors systemically suppress immune function and affect hematopoietic homeostasis. Safety profile for CDK9-specific modulation in mTORC1 hyperactivation contexts remains uncharacterized.
• mechanism(FZD7) — favorable (medium): FZD7-mediated Wnt→PI3K→mTOR signaling provides a coherent but partial compensatory brake on TSC2-KO hyperactivation. FZD7-KD could dampen one discrete upstream input to mTORC1, and Quiver's rank 2 internal data suggests empirical rescue activity. However, the mechanism is incomplete: canonical β-catenin signaling may have offsetting effects, and mTORC1 retains activation via amino-acid/GATOR and other parallel inputs.
• mechanism(VPS54) — neutral (low): VPS54's role in GARP-mediated retrograde trafficking could plausibly affect lysosomal composition and downstream mTORC1 signaling, but this pathway lacks direct published evidence in the mTORC1 literature. EMET identifies no mTORC1 link despite VPS54's known lysosomal-adjacent functions, creating the noted divergence with internal ranking. Mechanism is coherent in principle but unsupported in public literature.
• mechanism(BCL2) — neutral (medium): BCL2 interacts with mTOR pathway through BECN1-mediated autophagy; BCL2 knockdown could enhance autophagy flux in mTORC1-hyperactivated cells, potentially mitigating secondary cellular stress. However, BCL2 is a downstream effector that does not reverse constitutive mTORC1 activation itself, and loss of anti-apoptotic function creates competing cellular toxicity risk rather than mechanistic rescue.

data.text:
All layers in. The MTOR essentiality agent timed out (120s) — I won't re-run it; EMET already resolves MTOR unambiguously (on-target, pLI~1.0 constraint). The other four verdicts: **CDK9 = toxicity risk** (real but mechanism plausible), **FZD7 = favorable** (moat rank-2 corroborates the partial-brake mechanism), **VPS54 = neutral/low** (divergence stands — no published mechanism), **BCL2 = neutral** (downstream, doesn't reverse constitutive mTORC1).

## Scientific team read

**The biology gates this hard.** Only three genes have any literature-supported path to lowering mTORC1 drive:
- **MTOR** is the on-target node — knockdown unambiguously normalizes the phenotype (the positive control). Moat is silent only because mTOR isn't in the EP-neighbor table; mechanism overrides that. Caveat: severe constraint (pLI~1.0), pan-essential.
- **CDK9** — EMET's "most plausible rescue candidate," direct P-TEFb↔mTORC1 transcriptional intersection (PMID:31300006); semantic flags real on-target tox. Moat only rank 42 (weak corroboration).
- **FZD7** — plausible *partial* rescue (dampens one Wnt→PI3K→mTOR input; bidirectional, may worsen via β-catenin). This is the **best EMET+moat agreement**: moat rank 2 (d=0.20, the single strongest internal rescuer) corroborates a real mechanism.

**Divergences (Quiver alpha):** VPS54 (moat rank 5, d=0.21) and BCL2 (rank 8) and DPM2 (rank 14) score as strong internal rescuers but EMET finds no mTORC1 mechanism — genuine "moat sees what the literature can't" candidates for a wet-lab probe, but in an EMET-focused ranking they sit below the literature-backed hits.

**ESM calibration:** the ESM-nearest-to-TSC2 genes (SSU72 0.96, RPS3 0.95, SAP18 0.95) are precisely the no-effect/worsening genes — **ESM-to-target proximity does not track rescue in this set, so it is down-weighted to weak context only.**

**The against-tail:** KMT2D/SMARCE1 (loss *derepresses/activates* mTOR → worsen-leaning), RPS3/ACTR3 (pan-essential, knockdown lethal/collapse), and

plan_steps: step 1: Identify perturbation = TSC2 (mTORC1-hyperactivation); rank the 15 named genes by rescue-on-knockdown strength.
step 2: Quiver moat — TSC2 opposite-direction rescuers + --probe the exact 15-gene list (rescue/exacerbate/absent, rank + cosine_distance).
step 3: EMET batch dossier over all 15 genes vs TSC2 (captured, cited PMIDs, FOR-vs-AGAINST breadth incl. essentiality/constraint/expression).
step 4: ESM-2 embedding similarity of all 15 vs TSC2 (supporting context); confirmed proximity does NOT track rescue here, down-weighted.
step 5: One parallel semantic batch — essentiality/toxicity/mechanism on the control (MTOR), top candidate (CDK9) and the 3 biggest moat↔literature divergences (FZD7, VPS54, BCL2).
step 6: Rank by EMET-evidenced rescue strength, moat as corroboration; output JSON.

ranked_genes.mechanism: The on-target node itself: knocking down mTOR directly removes the kinase that TSC2 loss leaves de-repressed, normalizing mTORC1 output (S6K1/4E-BP1 axis).
ranked_genes.evidence_for: EMET: canonical on-target; KD/inhibition 'unambiguously normalizes' the TSC2-KO/mTORC1 phenotype; STRING direct interactions ~0.999 with RPTOR/RICTOR/RHEB/RPS6KB1/EIF4EBP1.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: Severe gene constraint (pLI~1.0), pan-essential — therapeutic-window/tolerability is the central risk (full KD lethal; clinically managed by partial inhibition, e.g. rapalogs). Moat-silent (not in EP-neighbor table) so no internal corroboration.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: near, 0.94 — but proximity is uninformative for rescue here; mechanism drives the call

ranked_genes.mechanism: CDK9/P-TEFb (transcription-elongation control) directly intersects the mTORC1 program; KD can throttle mTORC1-driven transcriptional output without hitting the kinase directly.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: EMET ranks CDK9 the MOST plausible knockdown-rescue candidate via a direct mTORC1 pathway intersection (PMID:31300006); moat present (rank 42) as weak corroboration.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: Semantic toxicity agent: broad on-target liability — CDK9 inhibition suppresses transcription elongation globally, hits immune/hematopoietic homeostasis; CNS-specific safety uncharacterized. Moat distance large (0.31), so internal signal is weak.

ranked_genes.mechanism: FZD7 feeds Wnt→PI3K→mTOR; KD removes one discrete upstream input to mTORC1, providing a partial brake (does not touch the TSC2-RHEB lesion directly).
ranked_genes.evidence_for: EMET: plausible partial rescue via Wnt/mTOR crosstalk (PMID:27531477); semantic mechanism agent 'favorable'; STRONGEST moat corroboration in the panel — rank 2, cosine-distance 0.20 (best EMET+moat agreement here).
ranked_genes.evidence_against: Crosstalk is bidirectional — canonical β-catenin effects may offset, and mTORC1 retains parallel amino-acid/GATOR inputs, so rescue is partial/context-dependent and could worsen in some contexts.

ranked_genes.mechanism: GARP-complex retrograde endosome→TGN tethering; a speculative lysosomal-composition→mTORC1 link only, with no published support.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Strong internal signal — moat rank 5 (cosine-distance 0.21), among the top internal rescuers; semantic notes a GARP→lysosome→mTORC1 path is coherent in principle.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: essentially NO published mTORC1 mechanistic link (PMID:26539077); semantic verdict neutral/low. Pure moat→literature DIVERGENCE — Quiver alpha worth a wet-lab probe, but unvalidated, so ranked below the literature-backed hits.

ranked_genes.mechanism: Anti-apoptotic effector engaged in BCL2-BECN1-mTOR autophagy crosstalk; KD might raise autophagy flux but does not reverse constitutive mTORC1 activation.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Moat rank 8 (cosine-distance 0.23) — a real internal rescue-direction signal; semantic notes possible autophagy-flux mitigation of secondary stress.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: downstream effector, does not address upstream mTORC1, may WORSEN (PMID:34161185); semantic neutral — loss of anti-apoptotic function adds competing toxicity rather than mechanistic rescue. Moat↔literature divergence, but EMET leans negative.

ranked_genes.mechanism: Dolichol-phosphate-mannose synthase regulatory subunit (N-glycosylation); any ER-stress→mTORC1 effect is purely speculative.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Moat rank 14 (cosine-distance 0.25) — present as a mid internal rescuer.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: no TSC2/mTORC1 mechanistic basis; most gnomAD-tolerant gene in the panel but ER-stress link speculative. Moat-only signal with no literature → low-confidence divergence.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: near, 0.94 — but proximity does not track rescue here; down-weighted

ranked_genes.mechanism: H3K4me3-reader / splicing chromatin factor; no route to the TSC2-RHEB-mTORC1 axis.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Moat rank 20 (cosine-distance 0.27) — weak internal presence only.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: highly constrained chromatin regulator, obesity-linked biology, NO TSC2/mTOR mechanistic connection; constraint raises tolerability concerns.

ranked_genes.mechanism: Sin3A/ASAP splicing-transcription factor; no mTORC1 mechanistic basis.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: no mTOR basis AND very high CNS expression → neurotoxicity/confounding concern on knockdown rather than rescue.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: near, 0.95 (ESM-3rd-nearest) yet no-effect — concrete proof ESM proximity ≠ rescue; down-weighted

ranked_genes.mechanism: H3K4 methyltransferase (MLL4); its LOSS can derepress mTOR, so knockdown trends toward worsening, not rescuing.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Moat present (rank 47, cosine-distance 0.33) — but weak and far.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: KMT2D/mTOR co-mutate in cancer, extreme gnomAD constraint, and loss can DEREPRESS mTOR (PMID:30641523) → ineffective-to-worsening; constraint also a tolerability flag.

ranked_genes.mechanism: Nuclear-receptor coactivator (PRIP); at most an indirect PI3K/AKT/mTOR transcriptional link, no direct rescue route.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: Moat present but weakest in-panel rescue-direction call (rank 48, cosine-distance 0.34).
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: judged ineffective for mTOR rescue, highly constrained; only a separate NLRP3-inflammasome effect plausible (off-axis liability).

ranked_genes.mechanism: RNA Pol II CTD phosphatase (transcription reinitiation/termination); no path to mTORC1.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: no TSC2/mTOR link in STRING/Reactome/literature (PMID:15125841); insertional-oncogenesis caution. No moat, no mechanism.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: near, 0.96 — the ESM-NEAREST gene to TSC2 yet no-effect: the clearest evidence ESM proximity does NOT track rescue; down-weighted

ranked_genes.mechanism: SWI/SNF (BAF) remodeler subunit acting as a tumor suppressor; its LOSS activates mTOR, so knockdown tends to worsen the phenotype.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: None on the rescue side — moat-silent.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: in clear-cell meningioma SMARCE1 loss ACTIVATES mTOR (PMID:39577862) → KD worsening; highly constrained tumor-suppressor with independent oncogenic risk.

ranked_genes.mechanism: Essential 40S ribosomal protein and a DOWNSTREAM effector of mTORC1-driven translation; knocking it down collapses translation rather than correcting the upstream lesion.
ranked_genes.evidence_for: None on the rescue side — moat-silent.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: essential ribosomal component; KD 'strongly worsening' (translation collapse), plus extra-ribosomal DNA-damage/NF-κB roles (PMID:32507070). Pan-essential = poor target.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: near, 0.95 (ESM-2nd-nearest) yet strongly worsening — reinforces ESM down-weighting

ranked_genes.mechanism: Arp3 (Arp2/3 branched-actin nucleator); pan-essential cytoskeletal gene with no mTORC1 rescue route — knockdown is broadly lethal.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: EMET: pan-essential (CCLE/DepMap), KD impairs cytokinesis/endocytosis globally, no mTOR mechanism, knockdown lethal.
ranked_genes.esm_signal: distant, 0.16 (ESM outlier; warm box timed out, cached) — n/a for rescue

ranked_genes.mechanism: 26S proteasome regulatory subunit (Rpn9); mTORC1 hyperactivation already stresses proteostasis, so KD drives proteostatic collapse — the opposite of rescue.
ranked_genes.evidence_against: The ONLY exacerbate-direction moat call in the panel (KD MIMICS/worsens the TSC2-KO signature, cosine-distance 0.40) AND EMET: pan-essential proteasome subunit, 'worsening' (UniProt:PSMD13-RPN9). Both layers agree it is against — placed at the bottom.

data.synthesis: EMET gates this panel hard: only three genes have any literature-supported route to lowering mTORC1 drive. MTOR is the unambiguous on-target rescue (#1, emet_support strong; KD normalizes the phenotype, STRING ~0.999 to RPTOR/RHEB/RPS6KB1/EIF4EBP1; its only real liability is severe constraint/pan-essentiality, and it is moat-silent only because it isn't in the EP-neighbor table). CDK9 (#2, PMID:31300006) is EMET's 'most plausible' candidate via a direct P-TEFb↔mTORC1 transcriptional intersection — tempered by a real on-target toxicity flag from the semantic toxicity agent and only weak moat corroboration (rank 42, d=0.31). FZD7 (#3, PMID:27531477) is a plausible PARTIAL brake on a Wnt→PI3K→mTOR input and shows the panel's best EMET+moat agreement — the single strongest internal rescuer (moat rank 2, cosine-distance 0.20) plus a 'favorable' semantic mechanism call. The notable DIVERGENCES are VPS54 (moat rank 5, d=0.21), BCL2 (rank 8) and DPM2 (rank 14): strong-to-mid internal rescue signals with NO published mTORC1 mechanism — genuine Quiver alpha for a wet-lab probe, but ranked below the literature-backed hits until evidence appears. The clearly-against tail is mechanistically coherent: KMT2D and SMARCE1 LOSS derepress/activate mTOR (so KD worsens), RPS3/ACTR3 are pan-essential (KD lethal/translation collapse), and PSMD13 sits at the very bottom as the only exacerbate-direction moat call AND a pan-essential proteasome subunit — the one gene where moat and EMET agree it is harmful. On ESM: the ESM-nearest-to-TSC2 genes (SSU72 0.96, RPS3 0.95, SAP18 0.95) are precisely the no-effect/worsening genes, so ESM-to-target proximity demonstrably does NOT track rescue in this set and was down-weighted to weak supporting context only (the MTOR essentiality semantic check timed out, but EMET already resolves MTOR's essentiality, so the ranking is unaffected).
